author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again in John v. 2, etc., on the five porches, where lay a great multitude of impotent folk, and of the pool of Siloa.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Again in John v. 2, etc., on the five porches, where lay a great multitude of impotent folk, and of the pool of Siloa.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Subjects strange neither to your ears nor hearts are now repeated: yet do they revive the affections of the hearer, and by repetition in some sort renew us: nor is it wearisome to hear what is well known already, for the words of the Lord are always sweet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The exposition of the sacred Scriptures is as the sacred Scriptures themselves: though they be well known, yet are they read to impress the remembrance of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so the exposition of them, though it be well known, is nevertheless to be repeated, that they who have forgotten it may be reminded, or they who chanced not to hear it may hear; and that with those who do retain what they are used to hear, it may by the repetition be brought to pass that they shall not be able to forget it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I remember that I have already spoken to you, Beloved, on this lesson of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet to repeat the same explanation to you is not wearisome, even as it was not wearisome to repeat the same Lesson to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul saith in a certain Epistle, “To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not wearisome, but for you it is necessary.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So too with myself to say the same things to you, to me is not wearisome, but for you it is safe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The five porches in which the infirm folk lay signify the Law, which was first given to the Jews and to the people of Israel by Moses the servant of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this Moses the minister of the Law wrote five books.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In relation therefore to the number of the books which he wrote, the five porches figured the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because the Law was not given to heal the infirm, but to discover and to manifest them; for so saith the Apostle, “For if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the Law; But the Scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe;” therefore in those porches the sick folk lay, but were not cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what saith he?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If there had been a law given which could have given life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore those porches which figured the Law could not cure the sick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some one will say to me, “Why then was it given?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul hath himself explained: “Scripture,” saith he, “hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these folk who were sick, thought themselves to be whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They received the Law, which they were not able to fulfil; they learnt in what disease they were, and they implored the Physician’s aid; they wished to be cured because they came to know they were in distress, which they would not have known if they had not been unable to fulfil the Law which had been given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man thought himself innocent, and from this very pride of false innocence became more mad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To tame this pride then and to lay it bare, the Law was given; not to deliver the sick, but to convince the proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then, Beloved; to this end was the Law given, to discover diseases, not to take them away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so then those sick folk who might have been sick in their own houses with greater privacy, if those five porches had not existed, were in those porches set forth to the eyes of all men, but were not by the porches cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law therefore was useful to discover sins, because that man being made more abundantly guilty by the transgression of the Law, might, having tamed his pride, implore the help of Him That pitieth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend to the Apostle; “The Law entered that sin might abound; but where sin abounded, grace hath much more abounded.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “The Law entered that sin might abound”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As in another place he saith, “For where there is no law, there is no transgression.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man may be called a sinner before the Law, a transgressor he cannot.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when he hath sinned, after that he hath received the Law, he is found not only a sinner, but a transgressor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as to sin is added transgression, therefore “hath sin abounded.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when sin abounds, human pride learns at length to submit itself, and to confess to God, and to say “I am weak.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To say to those words of the Psalm which none but the humbled soul saith, “I said, Lord, be merciful unto me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the weak soul then say this that is at least convinced by transgression, and not cured, but manifested by the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear too Paul himself showing thee, both that the Law is good, and yet that nothing but the grace of Christ delivereth from sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Law can prohibit and command; apply the medicine, that that which doth not allow a man to fulfil the Law, may be cured, it cannot, but grace only doeth that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Apostle saith, “For I delight in the Law of God after the inner man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, I see now that what the Law blames is evil, and what the Law commands is good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For I delight in the Law of God after the inner man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see another law in my members resisting the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity in the law of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This derived from the punishment of sin, from the propagation of death, from the condemnation of Adam, “resists the law of the mind, and brings it into captivity in the law of sin which is in the members.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was convinced; he received the Law, that he might be convinced: see now what profit it was to him that he was convinced.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the following words, “Wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give heed then.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those five porches were significative of the Law, bearing the sick, not healing them; discovering, not curing them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But who did cure the sick?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that descended into the pool.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when did the sick man descend into the pool?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Angel gave the sign by the moving of the water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus was that pool sanctified, for that the Angel came down and moved the water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men saw the water; and from the motion of the troubled water they understood the presence of the Angel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If any one then went down, he was cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then was not that sick man cured?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider his own words; “I have no man,” he says, “when the water is moved, to put me into the pool, but while I am coming, another steppeth down.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Couldest not thou then step down afterwards, if another step down before thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here it is shown us, that only one was cured at the moving of the water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever stepped down first, he alone was cured: but whoever stepped down afterwards, at that moving of the water was not cured, but waited till it was moved again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then does this mystery mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not without a meaning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Waters are put in the Apocalypse for a figure of peoples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when in the Apocalypse John saw many waters, he asked what it meant, and it was told him that they were peoples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The water then of the pool signified the people of the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as that people was held in by the five books of Moses in the Law, so that water too was enclosed by five porches.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When was the water troubled?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the people of the Jews was troubled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when was the people of the Jews troubled, but when the Lord Jesus Christ came?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord’s Passion was the troubling of the water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Jews were troubled when the Lord suffered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, what was just now read had relation to this troubling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Jews wished to kill Him, not only because He did these things on the sabbaths, but because He called Himself the Son of God, making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Christ called Himself the Son after one manner, in another was it said to men, “I said, Ye are Gods, and ye are all children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He had made Himself the Son of God in such sort as any man whatever may be called the son of God (for by the grace of God men are called sons of God); the Jews would not have been enraged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because they understand Him to call Himself the Son of God in another way, according to that, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” and according to what the Apostle saith, “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God;” they saw a man, and they were enraged, because He made Himself equal with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He well knew that He was equal, but wherein they saw not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that which they saw they wished to crucify; by That which they saw not, they were judged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did the Jews see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the Apostles also saw, when Philip said, “Show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what did the Jews not see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What not even the Apostles saw, when the Lord answered, “Have I been so long time with you, and yet have ye not known Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that seeth Me, seeth the Father also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then the Jews were not able to see This in Him, they held Him for a proud and ungodly man, making Himself equal with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here was a troubling, the water was troubled, the Angel had come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord is called also the “Angel of the Great Counsel,” in that He is the messenger of the Father’s will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Angel in Greek is in Latin “messenger”.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So you have the Lord saying that He announces to us the kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then had come, the “Angel of the Great Counsel,” but the Lord of all the Angels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Angel” on this account, because He took Flesh; the “Lord of Angels,” in that by “Him all things were made, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if all things, Angels too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore Himself was not made, because by Him all things were made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what was made, was not made without the operation of the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the flesh which became the mother of Christ, could not have been born, if it had not been created by the Word, which was afterwards born of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Jews then were troubled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why doeth He these things on the sabbath days?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And especially at those words of the Lord, “My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their carnal understanding of this, that God rested on the seventh day from all His works, “troubled them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is written in Genesis, and most excellently written it is, and on the best reasons.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they thinking that God as it were rested from fatigue on the seventh day after all, and that He therefore blessed it, because on it He was refreshed from His weariness, did not in their foolishness understand, that He who made all things by the Word, could not be wearied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them read, and tell me how could God be wearied, who said, “Let it be made, and it was made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To-day if a man could so do, as God did, how would he be wearied?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He said, “Let there be light, and the light was made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, “Let there be a firmament, and it was made:” if indeed He said, and it was not done, He was wearied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In another place briefly, “He spake, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who worketh thus, how doth He labour?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if He labour not, how doth He rest?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in that sabbath, in which it is said that God rested from all His works, in the Rest of God our rest was signified; because the sabbath of this world shall be, when the six ages shall have passed away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The six days as it were of the world are passing away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One day hath passed away, from Adam unto Noe; another from the deluge unto Abraham; the third from Abraham unto David; the fourth from David unto the carrying away into Babylon; the fifth from the carrying away into Babylon unto the advent of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the sixth day is in passing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are in the sixth age, in the sixth day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then be reformed after the image of God, because that on the sixth day man was made after the image of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What formation did then, let reformation do in us, and what creation did there, let creating-anew do in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After this day in which we now are, after this age, the rest which is promised to the saints and prefigured in those days, shall come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because in very truth too, after all things which He made in the world, He hath made nothing new in creation afterwards.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The creatures themselves shall be transformed and changed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For since the creatures were fashioned, nothing more has been added.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But nevertheless, if He who made did not rule the world, what is made would fall to ruin: He cannot but administer that which He hath made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then nothing hath been added to the creation, He is said to have rested from all His works; but because He doth not cease to govern what He made, rightly did the Lord say, “My Father worketh even hitherto.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He finished, He is said to have rested; for He finished His works, and hath added no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He governeth what He hath made; therefore He doth not cease to work.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But with the same facility that He made, with the same doth He govern.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For do not suppose, brethren, that when He created He did not labour, and that He laboureth in that He governeth: as in a ship, they labour who build the ship, and they who manage it labour too; for they are men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For with the same facility wherewith “He spake and they were made,” with the same facility and judgment doth He govern all things by the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not, because human affairs seem to be in disorder, fancy that there is no governance of human affairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all men are ordered in their proper places; but to every man it seems as though they have no order.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do thou only look to what thou wouldest wish to be; for as thou shalt wish to be, the Master knoweth where to place thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look at a painter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Before him are placed various colours, and he knows where to set each colour on.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Questionless the sinner hath chosen to be the black colour; does not then the Artist know where to place him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many parts does the painter finish off with the colour of black? how many ornaments does he make of it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With it he makes the hair, the beard, the eye-brows; he makes the face of white only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look then to that which thou wouldest wish to be; take no care where He may order thee who cannot err, He knoweth where to place thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so we see it happen by the common laws of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some man, for instance, has chosen to be a house-breaker: the law of the judge knows that he has acted contrary to the law: the law of the judge knows where to place him; and orders him most properly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He indeed has lived evilly; but not evilly has the law ordered him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From a house-breaker he will be sentenced to the mines; from the labour of such how great works are constructed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That condemned man’s punishment is the city’s ornament.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then God knoweth where to place thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not think that thou art disturbing the counsel of God, if thou art minded to be disorderly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth not He who knew how to create, know how to order thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Good were it for thee to strive for this, to be set in a good place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What was said of Judas by the Apostle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He went unto his own place.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the operation of course of Divine Providence, because by an evil will he chose to be evil, but God did not by ordering evil make it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because that evil man himself chose to be a sinner, he did what he would, and suffered what he would not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that he did what he would, his sin is discovered; in that he suffered what he would not, the order of God is praised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore have I said all this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ye, brethren, may understand what was most excellently said by the Lord Jesus Christ, “My Father worketh even hitherto.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that He doth not abandon the creature which He made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He said, “As He worketh, so do I also work.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this He at once signified that He was equal with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “My Father,” saith He, “worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their carnal sense touching the rest was troubled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they thought that the Lord being wearied rested, that He should work no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They hear, “My Father worketh even hitherto:” they are troubled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And I work:” He hath made Himself equal with God: they are troubled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But be not alarmed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The water is troubled, now the sick man is to be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore are they troubled, that the Lord may suffer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord doth suffer, the precious Blood is shed, the sinner is redeemed, grace is given to the sinner, to him that saith, “Wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The grace of God, through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how is he cured?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he step down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that pool was so made, that men should go down, and not come up to it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there might be pools of such a kind, so constructed, that men must go up to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why was this made in such a way that men must go down to it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the Lord’s Passion searches for the humble.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the humble go down, let him not be proud, if he wishes to be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why was it but “one”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the Church is only One throughout the world, unity is saved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When then one is made whole, unity is signified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By one understand unity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Depart not then from unity, if thou wouldest not be without a part in this saving cure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then does it mean that the man was in infirmity thirty-eight years?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know, brethren, that I have spoken of this already; but even those who read forget, how much more they who hear but seldom?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend therefore for a little while, Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the number forty, the accomplishment of righteousness is figured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The accomplishment of righteousness, in that we live here in labour, in toil, in self-restraint, in fastings, in watchings, in tribulations; this is the exercise of righteousness, to bear this present time, and to fast as it were from this world; not from the food of the body, which we do but seldom; but from the love of the world, which we ought to do always.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then fulfils the law who abstains from this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he cannot love that which is eternal, unless he shall cease to love that which is temporal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider a man’s love: think of it as, so to say, the hand of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it is holding anything, it cannot hold anything else.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that it may be able to hold what is given to it, it must leave go what it holds already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This I say, see how expressly I say it; “Whoso loveth the world cannot love God; he hath his hand engaged.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God saith to him, “Hold what I give.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will not leave go what he was holding; he cannot receive what is offered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have I said a man should not possess ought?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he is able, if perfection require this of him, let him not possess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If hindered by any necessity he is not able, let him possess, not be possessed; let him hold, not be held; let him be the lord of his possessions, not the slave; as saith the Apostle “However, brethren, the time is short; it remaineth that both they that have wives, be as though they had not; and they who buy, as though they possessed not; and they who rejoice, as though they rejoiced not; and they who weep, as though they wept not; and they who use this world, as though they used it not; for the fashion of this world passeth away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would have you be without carefulness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Do not love what thou dost possess in this world”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it not hold thine hand fast, by which God must be held.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not thy love be engaged, whereby thou canst make thy way to God, and cleave to Him who created thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt say and make answer to me, “Yea, God knows that I possess innocently what I have.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Temptation proves thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a troubling of thy possessions, and thou dost blaspheme.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but lately we were in such a case.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a troubling of thy possessions, and thou art not found what thou wast, and dost show that there is one thing in thy mouth to-day, and another in thy mouth yesterday.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And I would that thou wouldest only defend thine own even with vehemence; and not try to usurp with audacity another’s; and what is worse, to escape reprehension, maintain that what is another’s is thine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why need I say more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This I advise, this I say, Brethren, and as a brother advise; God bids, and I admonish because I am admonished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He alarmeth me, who doth not allow me to keep silence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He exacteth of me what He hath given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath given it to be laid out, not to be kept up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if I should keep it and hide it, He saith to me, “Thou wicked and slothful servant, wherefore gavest thou not My money to the exchangers, that at My coming I might require it with usury?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what will it profit me that I have lost nothing of that which I received?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is not enough for my Lord, He is covetous; but God’s covetousness is our salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is covetous, He looketh for His own money, He gathereth in His Own image.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shouldest have given,” saith He, “the money to the exchangers, that at My coming I might require it with usury.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if by any chance forgetfulness should make me fail of admonishing you, the temptations and tribulations at least which we are suffering, would be an admonition to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard at least the word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Blessed be the Lord and His glory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye are here gathered together, and are hanging on the word of God’s minister.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn not your attention to our flesh, by which the word is given out to you; for hungry men regard not the meanness of the dish, but the preciousness of the food.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is proving you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are gathered together, ye praise the word of God; temptation will prove in what manner ye hear it: ye will have the active business of life whereby your true character will be shown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so he who to-day is shouting with railings, was yesterday a ready listener.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore I forewarn; therefore I tell you, therefore I do not withhold it, my Brethren, that the time of questioning will come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord maketh question of the righteous and of the ungodly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This you know ye have sung, this have we sung together; “The Lord maketh question of the righteous and the ungodly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But he that loveth iniquity, hateth his own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in another place, “Into the thoughts of the ungodly there shall be questioning made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God doth not make question of thee there, where I question thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I question thy tongue, God questioneth thy thoughts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He knoweth how thou dost hear, and He knoweth how to require, Who ordereth me to give.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath wished me to be a dispenser, the requiring He hath reserved to Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To admonish, to teach, to rebuke, is ours; but to save, and to crown, or to condemn, and to cast into hell, is not ours; “But the Judge shall deliver to the officer, and the officer to the prison.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Verily I say unto thee, thou shalt not go out thence, till thou payest the last farthing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then return to our subject.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The perfection of righteousness is shown by the number forty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it to fulfil the number forty?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To restrain one’s self from the love of this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Restraint from temporal things, that they be not loved to our destruction, is, as it were, fasting from this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the Lord fasted forty days, and Moses, and Elias.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who gave His servants the power to fast forty days, could He not fast eighty or a hundred?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then did He not will to fast more than He had given His servants to do, but because in this number forty is the mystery of fasting, the restraint from this world?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this to say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the Apostle says; “The world is crucified to me, and I to the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then fulfils the number forty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what doth the Lord show?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That because Moses did this, this Elias, this Christ, that this both the Law, and the Prophets, and the Gospel, teach; that thou mayest not think that there is one thing in the Law, another in the Prophets, another in the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All Scripture teacheth thee nothing else, but restraint from the love of the world, that thy love may speed on to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As a figure that the Law teaches this, Moses fasted forty days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As a figure that the Prophets teach it, Elias fasted forty days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As a figure that the Gospel teaches it, the Lord fasted forty days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore in the mount too these three appeared, the Lord in the middle, Moses and Elias at the sides.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the Gospel itself receives testimony from the Law and the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why in the number forty is the perfection of righteousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Psalter it is said, “O God, I will sing a new song unto Thee, upon a psaltery of ten strings will I sing praises unto Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Which signifies the ten precepts of the Law, which the Lord came not to destroy, but to fulfil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Law itself throughout the whole world, it is evident, hath four quarters, the East, and West, South, and North, as the Scripture saith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hence the vessel which bare all the emblematic animals, which was exhibited to Peter, when he was told, “Kill and eat,” that it might be shown that the Gentiles should believe and enter into the body of the Church, just as what we eat entereth into our body, and which was let down from heaven by four corners (these are the four quarters of the world), showed that the whole world should believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in the number forty is restraint from the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the fulfilling of the Law: now the fulfilling of the Law is charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore before the Pasch we fast forty days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this time before the Pasch is the sign of this our toilsome life, wherein, in toils, and cares, and continence, we fulfil the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But afterwards we celebrate the Pasch, that is, the days of the Lord’s resurrection signifying our own resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore fifty days are celebrated; because the reward of the denarius is added to the forty, and it becomes fifty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is the reward a denarius?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have ye not read, how that they who were hired into the vineyard, whether at the first, or sixth, or the last hour, could only receive the denarius?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When to our righteousness shall be added its reward, we shall be in the number fifty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, and then shall we have none other occupation, save to praise God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore throughout those days we say, “Hallelujah.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Halleluiah is the praise of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this frail estate of mortality, in this fortieth number here, as though before the resurrection, let us groan in prayers, that we may sing praises then.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now is the time of longing, then will be the time of embracing and enjoying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not faint in the time of forty, that we may joy in the time of fifty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now who is he that fulfilleth the Law, but he that hath charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask the Apostle, “Charity is the fulfilling of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all the Law is fulfilled in one word, in that which is written, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the commandment of charity is twofold; “Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the great commandment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The other is like it; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the words of the Lord in the Gospel: “On these two commandments hang all the Law and the Prophets.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Without this twofold love the Law cannot be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As long as the Law is not fulfilled, there is infirmity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore he had two short, who was infirm thirty and eight years.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means, “had two short”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not fulfil these two commandments.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doth it profit that the rest is fulfilled, if those are not fulfilled?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou thirty-eight?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou have not those two, the rest will profit thee nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast two short, without which the rest avail not, if thou have not the two commandments which conduct unto salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I speak with the tongues of men and angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if I know all mysteries, and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if I distribute all my substance, and if I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Apostle’s words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All those things therefore which he mentioned are as it were the thirty-eight years; but because charity was not there, there was infirmity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From that infirmity who then shall make whole, but He who came to give charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because He came to give charity, and charity fulfilleth the Law, with good reason said He, “I came not to destroy the Law, but to fulfil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He cured the sick man, and told him to carry his couch, and go unto his house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so too He said to the sick of the palsy whom He cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it to carry our couch?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The pleasure of our flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where we lie in infirmity, is as it were our bed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who are cured master and carry it, are not by this flesh mastered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, thou whole one, master the frailness of thy flesh, that in the sign of the forty days’ fast from this world, thou mayest fulfill the number forty, for that He hath made that sick man whole, “Who came not to destroy the Law, but to fulfil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Having heard this, direct your heart to Godward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not deceive yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask yourselves then when it is well with you in the world ; then ask yourselves, whether ye love the world, or whether ye love it not; learn to let it go before ye are let go yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is to let it go?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not heartily to love it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whilst there is yet something with thee which thou must one day lose, and either in life or death let it go, it cannot be with thee always; whilst I say it is yet with thee, loosen thy love; be prepared for the will of God, hang upon God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hold thee fast to Him, whom thou canst not lose against thy will, that if it chance thee to lose these temporal things, thou mayest say, “The Lord gave, the Lord hath taken away, as it hath pleased the Lord, so is it done, blessed be the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if it chance, and God so wills it, that the things thou hast be with thee even to the last: for thy detachment from this life thou receivest the denarius, the fifty, and the perfection of blessedness cometh to pass in thee, when thou shalt sing Hallelujah.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Having these things which I have now brought forward in your memory, may they avail to overthrowing your love of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Evil is its friendship, deceitful, it makes a man the enemy of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Soon, in one single temptation, a man offendeth God, and becometh His enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay not then becometh His enemy; but is then discovered to have been His enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when he was loving and praising Him, he was an enemy; but he neither knew it himself, nor did others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Temptation came, the pulse is touched, and the fever discovered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then brethren, the love of the world, and the friendship of the world, make men the enemies of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it does not make good what it promises, it is a liar, and deceiveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore men never cease hoping in this world, and who attains to all he hopes for?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whereunto soever he attains, what he has attained to is forthwith disesteemed by him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Other things begin to be desired, other fond things are hoped for; and when they come, whatsoever it is that comes to thee, is disesteemed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hold thee fast then to God, for He can never be of light esteem, for nothing is more beautiful than He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For for this cause are these things disesteemed, because they cannot stand, because they are not what He is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For nought, O soul, sufficeth thee, save He who created thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever else thou apprehendest is wretched; for He Alone can suffice thee who made thee after His Own likeness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus it was expressly said, “Lord, show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There only can there be security; and where security can be, there in a certain sort will be insatiable satiety.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wilt neither be so satiated, as to wish to depart; nor will anything be wanting, as though thou couldest suffer want.
